channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Senegal gets CAS date for appeal to regain AFCON from Morocco
item.description: Senegal given October date by CAS for appeal to regain AFCON title won in chaotic final against Morocco in January.

item.title: World Cup hero Rodri to undergo back surgery before Man City’s new season
item.description: Spain and Man City midfielder Rodri will need surgery and rest before returning to the club, new coach Maresca said.

item.title: What to know about the White House Correspondents’ Dinner, take two
item.description: Perennially controversial dinner is back, with Trump set to attend, after a gunman derailed the proceedings in April.

item.title: Indian student protests: What’s next after Modi’s promise on exam leaks?
item.description: Leaders of the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) insist on the removal of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan.

item.title: Five killed in occupied West Bank after Israeli settlers attack
item.description: Four Palestinians and one Israeli were killed in a shooting in the occupied West Bank village of Till

item.title: US summons Malaysia envoy over Israel policy
item.description: Malaysia does not have any diplomatic ties with Israel and has long maintained its support for the Palestinian cause.

item.title: Bangladesh President Mohammed Shahabuddin resigns
item.description: Shahabuddin, 76, cited health reasons for leaving two years before his five-year term was meant to expire.

item.title: Spain declares national emergency over wildfires
item.description: At least 10,000 people have been evacuated following wildfires in the central region of Madrid.

item.title: Spain’s De la Fuente slams Argentina’s World Cup conduct as ‘unacceptable’
item.description: While Argentina faced criticism through World Cup 2026, post-final altercations are now being investigated.

item.title: At least 11 killed in Ukraine as Moscow and Kyiv continue to trade attacks
item.description: Six people also killed in Ukrainian attack on Russia’s Kirov, and Wildberries warehouses hit again.

item.title: Australia World Cup star Volpato faces speeding, cocaine charges
item.description: Cristian Volpato, who switched from Italy to Australia before World Cup 2026, failed a roadside drug test in Sydney.

item.title: Is the US-Saudi nuclear deal on, or in limbo?
item.description: Trump's last-minute demand for Saudi normalisation with Israel has complicated the already sensitive deal.

item.title: South African court pauses impeachment process against president
item.description: The 'Farmgate' scandal nearly cost President Cyril Ramaphosa the leadership of his African National Congress in 2022.

item.title: Andy Burnham’s Number 10 North changes the postcode, but not the politics
item.description: As Andy Burnham moves part of Downing Street to Manchester, Scotland and Wales are asking what it means for devolution.

item.title: Does this disappearing organ hold the secret of longevity?
item.description: Scientists are increasingly focused on the thymus, an immune gland that naturally becomes less active with age.

item.title: Israel’s illegal settlements are Europe’s credibility test
item.description: The EU has documented accelerating annexation. It must now use its collective power to confront it.

item.title: Four Palestinians killed as Israeli settlers raid occupied West Bank town
item.description: One Israeli was also killed near town of Tal in Nablus, according to Israel's Magen David Adom rescue services.

item.title: Germany appoint Klopp as ex-Liverpool coach sought to lift World Cup blues
item.description: Jurgen Klopp last coached in 2024 at Liverpool but was Red Bull's head of global football, leading Leipzig and New York.

item.title: Philippines accuses China of using water cannon on vessels for second day
item.description: The Philippine Coast Guard says 12 China Coast Guard vessels and three Chinese maritime militia vessels were involved.

item.title: Trump’s latest ‘forced labour tariffs’: Who’s been hit, how badly?
item.description: Countries accounting for almost all of the US's trade have been hit with tariffs between 10 and 12.5 percent.

item.title: Major fire after Ukrainian strike on St Petersburg warehouse
item.description: A major fire broke out at a Wildberries e-commerce warehouse in St Petersburg after a Ukrainian drone attack.

item.title: US attacks Iran for 13th consecutive night
item.description: The US military says it has completed its 13th night of strikes against Iran.

item.title: Sooryavanshi 18-ball fifty tops ‘tremendous’ India T20 win against Zimbabwe
item.description: India beat Zimbabwe by seven wickets as 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi scores maiden India half-century.

item.title: India’s ‘Cockroach’ leaders hold talks with gov’t as protests continue
item.description: Cockroach Janta Party protesters have stressed their demonstrations will continue until their demands have been met.

item.title: ICC prosecutor Karim Khan faces removal vote: What’s the case about?
item.description: ICC's 125 member states will vote by secret ballot Friday on the fate of Khan, who faces sexual misconduct allegations.
